title: Burning Fire demolish Young Killers team
bodyText:
FOOTBALL NEWS - Burning Fire (Umlilo) orchestrated a well-deserved victory, humiliating Young Killers FC with a whopping score of 6-0 in a Geolfa League encounter on Sunday 19 May at Thembalethu Stadium.


> Cristiano Williams, Thembisile Butse (brace), Siyasanga Bokodayo and Yamkela September contributed the six goals for Umlilo.


Zizipho Mtyukwini, the Burning Fire skipper, did a superb job at the back marshalling and leading the defence.

The game started at a high tempo with Burning Fire launching an attack on the right side of the field employing the pace of Cristiano Williams. Williams scored the first goal with a great strike.

With Young Killers pressing for the equaliser, Burning Fire still created the most chances into the box. Akhona Luke netted the second goal to make it 2-0 after his team mates' excellent work from the right side of the field.

Burning Fire left wing-back Sibusiso Ntuli used his speed to break through but his shot was denied by a Young Killers defender, forcing a corner kick. Thembisile Butse netted the third goal just before the half time break, making it 3-0.

The second half saw Burning Fire playing offensive football. Thembisile Butse netted his second goal and Siyasanga Bokodayo scored from a delightful pass after a free kick, upping their score to 5-0.

Burning Fire goalkeeper Lukhanyo Futha made a foul inside the box allowing a penalty for Young Killers, but Futha made a brilliant save, denying Young Killers their first goal. Burning Fire's Yamkela September delivered the final scorcher, upping their victory to a score of 6-0 with a strike just outside the box.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
